detaileddescription.para:
r
pointer to uninitialised struct
capacity
number of chunk slots; must be power-of-2, >= 2
chunk_samples
samples per chunk; must be > 0
Validate: r != NULL, chunk_samples > 0, capacity >= 2, capacity & (capacity-1) == 0. Return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG otherwise. Allocate capacity * chunk_samples * sizeof(ads1299_sample_t) bytes with malloc. Return ESP_ERR_NO_MEM if NULL. Set r->buf = <allocated>, r->capacity = capacity, r->chunk_samples = chunk_samples, r->mask = capacity - 1. Set r->head = 0, r->tail = 0. Return ESP_OK.

briefdescription.para: Creates and initialises an ADS1299 device handle.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to configuration structure containing pin maps.

briefdescription.para: Initialise hardware: configure GPIO outputs, add SPI device, create mutex, run the ADS1299 power-up sequence (reset, SDATAC, register config, test signal).

detaileddescription.para:
Prerequisites:
spi_bus_initialize() called by the application for cfg->spi_host
Power rails stable, VCAP1 will reach 1.1V within ~350ms

ESP_OK on success ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG if dev is NULL ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE if already initialised ESP_ERR_NO_MEM if mutex allocation fails propagated SPI / GPIO errors
Initialize the ADS1299 driver.

simplesect.para: ESP_OK on success, or an error code.

briefdescription.para: Stop any active continuous acquisition, remove SPI device, delete mutex, free all driver-owned memory.

detaileddescription.para: Safe to call from any task context.
Not valid while continuous mode is active (device must be in SDATAC).

memberdef.argsstring: (ads1299_t *dev, uint8_t start_reg, const uint8_t *data, size_t count)
memberdef.name: ads1299_write_registers

briefdescription.para: Burst register access — wraps WREG/RREG with count.
Send a raw SPI command byte (WAKEUP, STANDBY, RESET, START, STOP, RDATAC, SDATAC, RDATA).

detaileddescription.para: Use named wrappers below where possible.

briefdescription.para: Read raw 27-byte frame into caller-provided buffer.

detaileddescription.para:
Internally:
Computes chunk_samples = sample_rate_hz * chunk_duration_ms / 1000
Allocates two DMA-capable ping-pong buffers of chunk_samples * 27 bytes
Allocates ring buffer of ADS1299_RING_BUF_SLOTS * chunk_bytes
Installs falling-edge ISR on drdy_pin
Spawns handler task (task_core, task_priority)
ISR → xTaskNotifyFromISR → handler task → on_chunk callback
ads1299_enable_continuous_read() must be called before this. Register writes are not permitted while acquisition is active.

simplesect.para: ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE if not initialised or already running ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG if on_chunk is NULL ESP_ERR_NO_MEM if buffer allocation fails

listitem.para: Signals handler task to exit and waits for it
Frees ping-pong DMA buffers and ring buffer

detaileddescription.para: Blocks until the handler task has exited. Safe to call from any task.

briefdescription.para: Parse a raw 27-byte ADS1299 frame into an ads1299_sample_t.

detaileddescription.para: Exposed for unit testing. Not needed in normal application code.

parameterdescription.para: esp_timer_get_time() value to embed in the sample

briefdescription.para: Return the sample rate in Hz for a given ads1299_sample_rate_t enum value.

detaileddescription.para: Useful for computing chunk sizes and window dimensions in application code.
